title: Apology Letter Photo Upload and Share Page

requirements.text: Create a single-page website that lets a user attach/upload a photo of an apology letter and then generate a shareable link that can be sent to their boyfriend to view the uploaded photo.

quotes: create a website where i can attach a photo of an apology letter to my boyfruesnd

acceptanceCriteria: A user can select an image file (common formats like PNG/JPG) from their device and see a preview before submitting.
After submitting, the app displays a unique shareable URL for viewing the uploaded image.
Visiting the shareable URL in a fresh browser session loads and displays the uploaded image without requiring login.
If the uploaded file is not an image, the UI shows a clear validation error in English and prevents upload.

requirements.text: Implement backend storage and retrieval for uploaded apology-letter images using the single Motoko actor, including creation of a unique public ID per upload and a read endpoint to fetch the image by ID.

quotes: attach a photo of an apology letter

acceptanceCriteria: Backend provides a method to create/store an uploaded image and returns a unique ID.
Backend provides a query method to retrieve the stored image (bytes + content type) by ID.
If an ID does not exist, the backend returns a clear not-found result that the frontend handles gracefully.
All backend logic remains in the single main actor file; no additional backend services are introduced.

requirements.text: Create a dedicated 'View shared letter' route/page that reads the ID from the URL, fetches the image from the backend, and renders it responsively with basic loading and error states.
acceptanceCriteria: The view page shows a loading state while fetching.
If the ID is invalid/not found, the view page shows an English error message and a link back to the upload page.
The displayed image scales to fit mobile and desktop screens without overflowing.

requirements.text: Apply a coherent, creative visual theme appropriate for a personal apology-letter sharing page (avoid blue/purple as primary colors), and use it consistently across upload and view pages (typography, spacing, buttons, and layout).
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a consistent color palette, typography, and component styling across all pages.
Primary styling does not use blue/purple as the main theme colors.
Layout looks good on both mobile and desktop (no obvious overlapping/clipped UI).

requirements.text: Add generated static images as part of the UI theme (logo/illustration) and render them from frontend/public/assets/generated without routing images through the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images are stored under frontend/public/assets/generated and referenced directly by the frontend.
The upload page includes the generated illustration/logo in the header area.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in the main actor file).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose them instead).
Do not edit immutable hook files under frontend/src/hooks/* listed by the platform; integrate via new components/hooks as needed.
nonGoals: Sending the photo via email/SMS or integrating with messaging services.
Third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity is allowed, but not required for this request).
External databases or cloud storage providers.

required: Minimal line-art illustration of an envelope with a small heart, warm neutral palette (apology-illustration.dim_1200x400.png)
Simple monochrome site mark combining a small envelope icon and initials 'AL' (apology-logo.dim_256x256.png)
